cards.back: a method of data hiding in images where the least significant bit of each pixel is replaced with the data to be hidden
a method of data hiding in images where data is hidden in the least significant bits of the red color channel and the most significant bits of the green color channel
a method of data hiding in images where the original image is not altered, and the data to be hidden is embedded in the image without any degradation

cards.front: Embedding data in an image without altering its pixels
cards.back: a technique used to hide data in an image without changing its visual appearance, often used in image steganography
a measure of how well a steganographic method can hide data in an image without altering its visual appearance, often measured using metrics such as PSNR (Peak Signal-to-Noise Ratio)
